Developed by Abdessamad El Amrani, it implements a configuration-driven approach that exposes common malware analysis tools like 'strings', 'file', 'hexdump', 'objdump', and 'xxd' as structured commands with parameter validation. The server manages terminal processes with timeout handling and output buffering, making it ideal for security researchers who need to analyze suspicious files directly within Claude Desktop without switching to external tools.
fields.author: @abdessamad-elamrani

fields.name: DRF Docs
fields.description: Serves Django REST Framework API documentation via the Model Context Protocol, enabling AI coding agents to browse endpoints, schemas, and authentication methods. Provides tools for searching endpoints, generating request/response examples, and producing integration code in JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, and cURL. Supports drf-spectacular, drf-yasg, and DRF's built-in schema generation with both stdio and streamable-http transports.
fields.author: @abdulkhalek-1

fields.name: Code Executor
fields.description: Universal code execution MCP server providing secure sandboxed execution of TypeScript and Python code with progressive disclosure capabilities achieving 98% token savings. Generates ergonomic wrapper functions for MCP tools instead of exposing raw schemas. Features Docker-based security with AppArmor profiles, seccomp filtering, and resource limits. Real-time output streaming via WebSocket, comprehensive audit logging, and automatic wrapper generation with sensible defaults and state management. Uses Deno for TypeScript execution with STDIO and HTTP MCP transports.
fields.author: @aberemia24

fields.name: RetellAI Voice Services
fields.description: The RetellAI MCP server provides AI assistants with direct access to RetellAI's voice services for creating and managing phone conversations. Built by abhaybabbar, this implementation exposes tools for call management, agent configuration, phone number provisioning, and voice selection through the Model Context Protocol. The server connects to RetellAI's API using a provided API key and transforms requests and responses between MCP format and RetellAI's native format. It's particularly valuable for applications that need to initiate outbound calls, handle inbound calls, or create voice agents with specific personalities and capabilities for tasks like automated customer service, appointment scheduling, or information gathering.
fields.author: @abhaybabbar

fields.name: Email Validator
fields.description: MCP-Email-Verify provides a tool for validating email addresses using the Abstract API service. The server exposes a single function that checks email validity, deliverability, and quality through comprehensive validation including format checking, SMTP verification, and identification of disposable or role-based addresses. This implementation is particularly useful for applications requiring email validation to reduce bounces, prevent fake signups, and ensure data quality in forms or user registration flows.
fields.author: @abhi5h3k

fields.name: URL2SNAP (Website Screenshot)
fields.description: URL2SNAP is an MCP server that captures screenshots of websites through the Abstract API. It provides a simple tool that takes a URL as input and returns a local URL to access the captured screenshot. The implementation handles API requests, image processing, and local file storage, making it useful for applications that need to visualize web content without requiring users to visit the actual websites. The server runs on port 8011 and saves screenshots to a local directory for easy access.
fields.author: @abhi5h3k

fields.name: Promptheus
fields.description: Provides intelligent prompt refinement through an adaptive questioning workflow that detects task types and optimizes prompts accordingly. Supports multiple AI providers including Google Gemini, Anthropic Claude, OpenAI, Groq, Qwen, and GLM. Features interactive and structured question modes, session history management, and privacy-preserving telemetry.
fields.author: @abhichandra21

fields.name: BTCPayServer
fields.description: A BTCPayServer MCP integration built by Abhijay007 that provides complete API coverage for Bitcoin payment processing, store management, and Lightning Network operations through a service-based architecture. The implementation organizes BTCPayServer's extensive API into 23 modular services covering payments (invoices, payment requests, pull payments), store operations (email, payment methods, payouts, rates, users, wallets), Lightning functionality (internal operations, store configuration, address management), and system administration (webhooks, notifications, API keys, user management). Built with TypeScript and error handling, it uses a discovery pattern where users can explore available services and methods before executing operations, making it ideal for e-commerce integrations, automated payment processing, multi-store management, and Bitcoin-native business applications.
fields.author: @abhijay007

fields.name: Kubernetes
fields.description: The MCP Kubernetes Server provides a bridge between Large Language Models and Kubernetes clusters through a set of specialized tools. Built with Python using the FastMCP framework, it wraps kubectl commands to enable operations like retrieving pod information, managing deployments, switching contexts, and troubleshooting cluster issues. The server requires a configured kubectl environment and can be integrated with Claude Desktop for seamless natural language interactions with Kubernetes resources. It's particularly valuable for DevOps workflows that benefit from conversational interfaces to Kubernetes, allowing users to create, scale, and monitor resources without remembering exact kubectl syntax.
fields.author: @abhijeetka

fields.name: SQLite Database
fields.description: A SQLite database interaction server that provides secure query execution and schema inspection capabilities for local SQLite databases. Built with FastMCP and aiosqlite, it exposes tools for retrieving database table structures and running SQL operations including SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE statements. The server automatically handles database connections, commits transactions for write operations, and returns structured JSON responses.
fields.author: @abhinab-handique

fields.name: Think Tool
fields.description: Think Tool server provides a structured way for AI assistants to maintain an explicit thought process through a session. It exposes tools for recording thoughts, retrieving the thought history, and clearing the thought log when needed. Built with TypeScript and the MCP protocol, it helps AIs break down complex reasoning tasks, verify policy compliance, and analyze tool outputs by maintaining a persistent memory of their thinking process.
fields.author: @abhinav-mangla

fields.name: System Resource Monitor
fields.description: A Model Context Protocol server for real-time system monitoring, providing CPU, memory, disk, network, battery, and internet speed metrics via the systeminformation library. Developed by Abhinav, this implementation exposes both a resource endpoint for comprehensive system snapshots and individual tools for targeted metrics retrieval. The server uses multi-source speed tests from various providers to deliver reliable internet performance measurements, making it particularly useful for diagnosing performance issues, monitoring resource utilization, and tracking network connectivity without leaving the AI assistant conversation.
fields.author: @abhinav7895

fields.name: Fermat (Mathematical Computing)
fields.description: This MCP server provides AI assistants with mathematical computation and plotting capabilities through three specialized modules: matplotlib for data visualization, NumPy for numerical operations, and SymPy for symbolic mathematics, built using Python with FastMCP and modular architecture. The implementation offers comprehensive tools for creating bar charts with customizable orientations and styling, performing matrix operations and statistical calculations, solving algebraic equations and calculus problems, and handling symbolic computation tasks like differentiation and integration. Built with unified function interfaces, JSON-serializable outputs, and prefix-based module organization, it serves data scientists needing conversational access to mathematical tools, educators requiring AI-assisted problem solving across numerical and symbolic domains, and researchers wanting to integrate mathematical computation into automated workflows without direct library interaction.
fields.author: @abhiphile

fields.name: Azure Cosmos DB
fields.description: This MCP server implementation provides tools for interacting with Azure Cosmos DB databases using Go. Built with the Azure SDK for Go, it enables AI assistants to perform common Cosmos DB operations including listing databases and containers, executing SQL queries, reading container metadata, creating containers, and managing items. The implementation supports both key-based authentication and Azure managed identities, making it flexible for different deployment scenarios. It's particularly useful for applications that need to integrate AI capabilities with document database operations, allowing natural language interaction with Cosmos DB resources without requiring users to write complex database code.
fields.author: @abhirockzz

fields.name: Azure Data Explorer (Kusto)
fields.description: An MCP server for Azure Data Explorer (Kusto) that enables AI assistants to interact with Kusto clusters through the Azure SDK for Go. It provides tools for listing databases, tables, retrieving table schemas, and executing KQL queries against Azure Data Explorer databases. The implementation uses DefaultAzureCredential for authentication, making it secure and simple to use with local developer credentials or service principals. Ideal for data analysts and engineers who want to leverage AI assistants for exploring and analyzing data in Azure Data Explorer clusters.
fields.author: @abhirockzz

fields.name: DeepRepo
fields.description: A production-grade Python library for performing retrieval-augmented generation on local codebases. Provides tools for codebase ingestion, semantic querying, and similarity-based code discovery. Supports multiple AI providers including Ollama, HuggingFace, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini.
fields.author: @abhishek2432001

fields.name: Contentstack App
fields.description: Contentstack App provides tools and knowledge resources for AI coding agents to build, migrate, and audit Contentstack Marketplace apps. It includes scaffolding tools (cs_preflight, cs_plan, cs_scaffold, cs_verify), SDK documentation resources, and pattern guides that prevent common AI mistakes such as hallucinated API methods or missing required UI components. The server is available as a hosted remote endpoint deployable via Contentstack Launch, Vercel, or any Node.js host using Streamable HTTP transport.
fields.author: @abhishek305

fields.name: Airflow
fields.description: Airflow MCP Server provides a bridge between Claude and Apache Airflow, enabling direct interaction with Airflow workflows through the API. Built by Abhishek Bhakat, it supports both read-only (safe) and full-access (unsafe) operation modes, allowing users to list DAGs, check statuses, trigger runs, and manage Airflow resources without leaving their conversation interface. The implementation parses the Airflow OpenAPI specification to dynamically generate tools, supports both token and cookie-based authentication, and handles parameter validation - making it particularly valuable for DevOps teams who need to monitor and control workflow orchestration through natural language.
fields.author: @abhishekbhakat

fields.name: Code Assist
fields.description: This MCP server implementation, developed for code modification and generation tasks, provides a set of tools for AI assistants to create, modify, delete, and rewrite code files. Built with Python and leveraging libraries like aiofiles, gitpython, and xmlschema, it offers a standardized XML-based interface for instructing code changes. The implementation focuses on file operations, search-and-replace modifications, and version control integration, making it accessible for AI models to perform complex code editing tasks. It's particularly useful for automated code refactoring, codebase maintenance, or integrating AI-driven code generation into existing projects, allowing assistants to manipulate code without requiring deep knowledge of file system operations or version control complexities.
fields.author: @abhishekbhakat

fields.name: Sonic Pi
fields.description: A Model Context Protocol server that allows AI assistants to interact with Sonic Pi through OSC messages, enabling programmatic music creation and sound manipulation. The server provides tools for playing individual notes with customizable synth parameters and executing arbitrary Sonic Pi code, making it valuable for AI-assisted music composition, sound design, and educational scenarios. Developed by Abhishek Jairath, it works with any MCP-compatible client like Claude Desktop or Cursor, and requires Sonic Pi to be running with a specific OSC handler code to receive and execute the commands.
fields.author: @abhishekjairath

fields.name: Todoist
fields.description: This MCP server, developed by abhiz123, enables natural language task management in Todoist through AI assistants like Claude. It provides tools for creating, retrieving, updating, completing, and deleting tasks using conversational language. The server leverages Todoist's API to translate natural language commands into precise Todoist operations, supporting features like smart task search, flexible filtering, and rich task details. By bridging AI capabilities with Todoist's task management system, this implementation enhances productivity workflows, allowing users to manage their tasks more intuitively. It is particularly useful for scenarios requiring hands-free task management, quick capture of ideas, or AI-assisted organization of complex projects within Todoist.
fields.author: @abhiz123

fields.name: Ableton Live
fields.description: This MCP server provides real-time control and automation of Ableton Live through a dual-component architecture consisting of a Python Remote Script that runs inside Ableton Live and an external MCP server that communicates via socket connections. Built by Siddharth Ahuja, it offers 16 tools covering session management (track creation, tempo control), MIDI clip operations (note insertion, playback control), and browser integration for loading instruments and effects, enabling AI assistants to compose music, automate production workflows, and manipulate Live sessions programmatically. The implementation features thread-safe command processing, comprehensive error handling with validation, and support for both real-time playback control and detailed session introspection, making it valuable for music producers, composers, and developers who want AI assistance in creating beats, arranging tracks, loading sounds, and automating repetitive studio tasks within Ableton Live.
fields.author: @chaudepark

fields.name: Google Docs
fields.description: Google Docs MCP Server provides a bridge between Claude Desktop (or other MCP clients) and Google Docs, enabling AI assistants to read document content, append text, and apply formatting to specific text within documents. Built with TypeScript and the fastmcp library, it handles OAuth 2.0 authentication with Google's APIs and offers three main tools: readGoogleDoc for fetching document content, appendToGoogleDoc for adding text to documents, and formatMatchingText for applying styling (bold, color, etc.) to specific text instances. The server is designed for users who want their AI assistants to interact directly with their Google Documents, making it useful for collaborative writing, document analysis, or automated content generation.
fields.author: @a-bonus

fields.name: Alfresco Content Services
fields.description: This Alfresco MCP Proof of Concept, developed by aborroy, demonstrates integration of Alfresco's content management capabilities with AI applications using the Model Context Protocol. Built using Java and Node.js, it combines a standard Alfresco deployment with custom MCP client and server components. The implementation leverages Spring AI and the TypeScript SDK to connect Alfresco's document management features to AI workflows. It's particularly useful for organizations looking to enhance their Alfresco-based content repositories with AI capabilities, enabling use cases like intelligent document processing, automated metadata extraction, and content-aware chatbots without extensive custom development.
fields.author: @aborroy

fields.name: LedFX
fields.description: Provides comprehensive control over LedFX LED lighting systems through a TypeScript-based MCP server that bridges natural language commands to the LedFX REST API. Features an extensive color library with named colors and gradients, intelligent scene parsing that converts descriptions into proper effect configurations, and local SQLite storage for custom palettes and playlists. Includes effect recommendations based on mood and context, detailed explanations of LedFX concepts like virtuals and audio-reactive effects, and support for all major LedFX operations including device management, effect control, and scene creation.
fields.author: @abossard

fields.name: Ab Ovo
fields.description: Enables email-to-web publishing through the ABOVO.co platform. Sends content to POST@abovo.co and receives a permanent public URL in seconds. Supports personal pages, group pages with moderation, and handles text, HTML, and file attachments. No API key or signup required as SMTP serves as the API. Includes a remote streamable HTTP endpoint for direct integration without local installation.
fields.author: @seanfenlon

fields.name: AbraFlexi
fields.description: Provides full CRUD access to the AbraFlexi (formerly FlexiBee) accounting platform through MCP-compatible tools. Supports invoice management, contact and product administration, bank transaction handling, and generic evidence queries. Features read-only mode for safe monitoring and multiple transport options.
fields.author: @vitexsoftware

fields.name: IBM AS/400 iSeries
fields.description: A Spring Boot-based MCP server that enables AI assistants to interact with IBM AS/400 and iSeries mainframe systems through RTE (Remote Terminal Emulator) protocols including TN5250, TN3270, and VT420. Built by Abstracta using the jmeter-bzm-rte library, it provides a configurable YAML-based approach for defining connection parameters and automated terminal interaction flows, allowing users to execute mainframe login sequences and retrieve screen output as text. Particularly valuable for modernizing legacy system interactions, enabling AI assistants to perform mainframe operations like user authentication and menu navigation without requiring direct terminal access or specialized mainframe knowledge.
fields.author: @abstracta

fields.name: Codex WhatsApp Relay
fields.description: Links a local WhatsApp account to OpenAI Codex, enabling message reading, sending, and phone-based control of Codex sessions. Features include QR-code authentication, voice note transcription with local Parakeet models, session switching between Codex threads, and configurable permission levels from read-only to full access.
fields.author: @abuiles

fields.name: National Weather Service
fields.description: Built by Abukstech using TypeScript and the MCP SDK, this server integrates directly with the National Weather Service API to fetch real-time weather information including alert severity levels, affected areas, temperature forecasts, wind conditions, and short-term weather outlooks. It offers two core tools for retrieving weather alerts by state and detailed forecasts by geographic coordinates. The implementation is limited to US locations only due to NWS API constraints, making it valuable for applications requiring reliable government weather data for domestic weather monitoring, alert systems, and location-based weather services.
fields.author: @abukstech

fields.name: Gatherings (Expense Sharing)
fields.description: Gatherings MCP Server provides a tool for managing shared expenses in social events, helping users track costs, calculate reimbursements, and settle balances between friends. Built with a TypeScript wrapper around a Python backend using SQLAlchemy for data persistence, it offers features like creating gatherings, adding expenses, recording payments, and calculating who owes what to whom. The server uses a non-standard architecture where the Node.js component translates MCP requests to command-line calls to the Python implementation, making it suitable for personal expense tracking in group settings while acknowledging areas for architectural improvement.
fields.author: @abutbul

fields.name: Gatherings (Expense Sharing)
fields.description: This MCP server implementation provides a robust expense-sharing solution for social gatherings. Built with Python and SQLAlchemy, it offers tools for creating gatherings, managing members, tracking expenses, calculating reimbursements, and generating detailed payment summaries. The server employs a structured database model and includes features like member renaming and gathering closure. It's designed for use cases such as splitting costs for group trips, dinners, or any event where expenses need to be fairly divided among participants.
fields.author: @abutbul

fields.name: Reading Companion
fields.description: A reading companion that helps users build and track personalized reading goals through four sequential stages. The Interviewer conducts conversational interviews to establish reading profiles, the Context Builder analyzes profiles to extract deeper patterns, the Syllabus Builder generates curated book recommendations organized by domain with thoughtful sequencing, and the Reflection Partner facilitates post-reading reflection and learning synthesis. Stores all data locally in the user's home directory and generates markdown files for easy review.
fields.author: @aby0

fields.name: Kagi Search
fields.description: Developed by ac3xx, this server provides an interface for language models to perform web searches using Kagi's search engine. It enables AI agents to query Kagi's API and retrieve search results through a controlled MCP server. Built in TypeScript, the implementation currently focuses on basic web search functionality, with plans to expand to other Kagi features like text summarization and enriched news results. The server handles API authentication and request formatting. By leveraging Kagi's privacy-focused search capabilities, this implementation is particularly useful for AI assistants needing to access current web information, conduct research, or provide search results while respecting user privacy preferences.
fields.author: @ac3xx

fields.name: Apidog Tests
fields.description: Integrates with the Apidog testing platform to manage test cases, scenarios, suites, and test data. Agents can create, list, update, and execute API tests through the Apidog API. Requires an Apidog access token and project configuration for authentication.
fields.author: @acabala

fields.name: Aegis
fields.description: Aegis is a Python middleware that sits between AI agents and their actions, providing policy-based governance. It evaluates every action against YAML-defined rules with glob matching, enforces four-tier risk classification (low/medium/high/critical), and gates execution through configurable approval handlers including CLI, Slack, Discord, Telegram, and webhooks. The MCP adapter wraps Model Context Protocol tool calls with the same governance pipeline. Additional capabilities include behavioral anomaly detection, compliance reporting, cryptographic audit chains, RBAC, and multi-tenant isolation.
fields.author: @acacian

fields.name: Academic Author Network
fields.description: This MCP server provides academic author network analysis by aggregating data from multiple scholarly sources including Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, Crossref, and Google Scholar to find co-authors and research keywords for academic researchers. Built by Alperen Kocyigit, it offers two core tools: co-author discovery that identifies research collaborators with collaboration frequency counts, and keyword extraction that scrapes Google Scholar profiles to retrieve research interests and areas of expertise. The implementation features intelligent rate limiting across APIs, caching to reduce redundant requests, parallel data fetching for performance, and respectful web scraping with delays, making it valuable for academic research analysis, collaboration mapping, and understanding research networks in scholarly communities.
fields.author: @alperenkocyigit

fields.name: BioMCP
fields.description:
BioMCP is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server designed to enhance large language models with protein structure analysis capabilities. It provides tools for analyzing protein active sites and searching for disease-related proteins by interfacing with established protein databases.

Future work will be centered around enabling agents to utilize the BioMCP.
fields.author: @acashmoney

fields.name: AceDataCloud Flux
fields.description: Generate and edit images using Flux AI models through the AceDataCloud unified API platform. Supports multiple Flux model variants including flux-dev, flux-pro, and flux-kontext. Features text-to-image generation, context-aware image editing, task management, and model selection. Available as a hosted service or self-hosted via PyPI.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: AceDataCloud Luma
fields.description: Generate AI videos using Luma Dream Machine models through the AceDataCloud unified API platform. Supports text-to-video and image-to-video generation with customizable parameters. Available as a hosted service or self-hosted via PyPI.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: AceDataCloud Midjourney
fields.description: Generate and edit images using Midjourney AI through the AceDataCloud unified API platform. Supports image generation, variation, upscaling, and editing operations. Available as a hosted service or self-hosted via PyPI.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: AceDataCloud NanoBanana
fields.description: Generate and edit images using NanoBanana AI models through the AceDataCloud unified API platform. Supports text-to-image generation and image editing with customizable parameters. Available as a hosted service or self-hosted via PyPI.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: AceDataCloud Seedance
fields.description: Generate AI videos using ByteDance Seedance models through the AceDataCloud unified API platform. Supports text-to-video and image-to-video generation. Available as a hosted service or self-hosted via PyPI.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: AceDataCloud Seedream
fields.description: Generate images using ByteDance Seedream AI models through the AceDataCloud unified API platform. Supports text-to-image generation with customizable parameters. Available via PyPI for self-hosted deployments.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: AceDataCloud SERP
fields.description: Access Google search results via SERP API through the AceDataCloud unified API platform. Retrieve search engine results pages with structured data extraction. Available as a hosted service or self-hosted via PyPI.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: AceDataCloud ShortURL
fields.description: Shorten and manage URLs through the AceDataCloud unified API platform. Create short links, track click analytics, and manage URL redirects. Available as a hosted service or self-hosted via PyPI.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: AceDataCloud Sora
fields.description: Generate AI videos using OpenAI Sora models through the AceDataCloud unified API platform. Supports text-to-video generation with customizable parameters. Available as a hosted service or self-hosted via PyPI.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: AceDataCloud Suno
fields.description: Generate AI music, lyrics, and covers using Suno models through the AceDataCloud unified API platform. Supports text-to-music generation, lyric creation, and cover song production. Available as a hosted service or self-hosted via PyPI.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: AceDataCloud Veo
fields.description: Generate AI videos using Google Veo models through the AceDataCloud unified API platform. Supports text-to-video generation with customizable parameters. Available via PyPI for self-hosted deployments.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: AceDataCloud Hailuo
fields.description: AI video generation through the Hailuo (MiniMax) model on the AceDataCloud platform, supporting text-to-video creation. Requires an AceDataCloud API token from platform.acedata.cloud.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: AceDataCloud Kling
fields.description: AI video generation through the Kling model on the AceDataCloud platform, supporting text-to-video and image-to-video creation. Requires an AceDataCloud API token from platform.acedata.cloud.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: AceDataCloud Producer
fields.description: AI music generation through the Producer/Riffusion model on the AceDataCloud platform, supporting text-to-music composition. Requires an AceDataCloud API token from platform.acedata.cloud.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: AceDataCloud Wan
fields.description: AI video generation through the Wan model on the AceDataCloud platform, supporting text-to-video and image-to-video creation. Requires an AceDataCloud API token from platform.acedata.cloud.
fields.author: @acedatacloud

fields.name: Synthetic Search
fields.description: Provides web search capabilities through the Synthetic Search API, returning structured results with titles, URLs, descriptions, and publication dates for queries up to 400 characters. Integrates with the Synthetic Search API as an MCP server to enable AI assistants to perform real-time web searches.
fields.author: @acheong08

fields.name: Browse
fields.description: Gives AI assistants controlled internet access through three tools: web search (via DuckDuckGo, Tavily, or Serper), page fetching with HTML-to-text conversion, and file downloading. Built in Go with a comprehensive security stack including JWT validation against JWKS endpoints, CEL expression-based tool and URL policies, and access logging with header redaction. Supports both stdio and HTTP transport modes with Docker deployment, making it suitable for enterprise environments requiring fine-grained control over which domains and tools each user can access.
fields.author: @achetronic

fields.name: Filesystem
fields.description: Go-based filesystem server with enterprise security features including role-based access control using CEL expressions, JWT-based authentication with OAuth2 integration, and a comprehensive undo system for file operations. Supports both stdio and HTTP transports, offers process management for background command execution, scratch storage, and system information retrieval. Designed for development workflows and system administration tasks requiring fine-grained permission control over file operations.
fields.author: @achetronic

fields.name: Home Assistant (Achetronic)
fields.description: A Go-based MCP server that bridges AI assistants with Home Assistant smart home systems, providing comprehensive control over IoT devices, automation management, and system monitoring. Offers tools for entity management (querying states, controlling devices), service calls, system insights (domain summaries, error logs), and troubleshooting capabilities. Supports both local stdio transport for desktop AI clients and HTTP transport for remote access, with production-ready features including OAuth authentication, JWT validation, Docker containerization, and Kubernetes deployment via Helm charts.
fields.author: @achetronic

fields.name: DELPHI Oracle
fields.description: DELPHI is an autonomous oracle system for the agent economy, publishing real-time intelligence signals across five categories: security exploits and vulnerabilities, market signals including yield and price data, ecosystem developments tracking new agents and funding, API health monitoring, and synthesized research insights. Signals update every 15 minutes and are accessed via x402 USDC micropayments on Base Mainnet.
fields.author: @achilliesbot

fields.name: Safari Automation
fields.description: Enables AI agents to automate Safari browser tasks through 80 tools covering navigation, form input, screenshots, network monitoring, accessibility, and data extraction. Runs natively on macOS using AppleScript with a persistent process for low-latency commands. Maintains existing browser sessions and operates in the background without stealing window focus.
fields.author: @achiya-automation

fields.name: Achriom
fields.description: Integrates with Achriom, a personal media collection platform for tracking and managing books, movies, albums, TV shows, and anime. Includes ratings, notes, reading/watching history, YouTube video search, Apple Music previews, and AI-powered collection analysis.
fields.author: @achriom

fields.name: Microsoft Fabric
fields.description: Fabric MCP is a Python-based server for interacting with Microsoft Fabric APIs, providing tools to manage and explore data assets in the Fabric ecosystem. It enables users to list workspaces, lakehouses, warehouses, tables, semantic models, and reports, as well as retrieve detailed schema information for Delta tables. Built with Azure authentication integration, the server maintains context across sessions using a TTL cache and formats results as markdown for readability. This implementation is particularly valuable for data engineers and analysts who need to explore and document their Fabric resources without leaving their AI assistant interface.
fields.author: @datumnova

fields.name: Fetch JSONPath
fields.description: A Python-based MCP server built by ackness that provides HTTP data fetching and JSON extraction capabilities using JSONPath patterns. The implementation offers four core tools: single and batch JSON extraction from URLs with configurable JSONPath filtering, and single and batch raw text content retrieval, all with concurrent processing for improved performance. Features comprehensive HTTP client configuration through environment variables including custom headers, proxy support, SSL verification controls, and timeout settings, along with robust error handling and validation using Pydantic models. Designed for AI assistants that need to programmatically fetch and parse structured data from web APIs or extract specific fields from JSON responses at scale.
fields.author: @ackness

fields.name: ACP Bridge
fields.description: This MCP server bridges Agent Communication Protocol (ACP) agents with MCP clients, enabling AI assistants to access and orchestrate complex multi-agent workflows through a unified interface. Built by GongRzhe using FastMCP and the ACP SDK, it provides intelligent agent discovery and routing, multi-modal message conversion between ACP's rich content types and MCP formats, and support for synchronous, asynchronous, and streaming execution modes with interactive agent patterns that handle await/resume workflows. The implementation features automatic agent discovery from ACP servers, smart routing based on configurable keyword strategies, run orchestration with session management, and Docker deployment options, making it valuable for users who want to leverage sophisticated ACP agent networks through familiar MCP clients like Claude Desktop while maintaining the full expressiveness of agent-to-agent communication patterns.
fields.author: @gongrzhe

fields.name: Acrolinx NextGen
fields.description: This MCP server integrates with Acrolinx's NextGen API to provide AI assistants with professional text analysis and improvement capabilities, built by Acrolinx using TypeScript with comprehensive workflow management including retry logic, timeout handling, and polling mechanisms. The implementation offers four core tools: automated text rewriting that transforms content while maintaining style guide compliance, quality analysis that provides detailed scores across grammar/clarity/tone dimensions, targeted editing suggestions for manual improvements, and workflow status checking for asynchronous operations. Supporting multiple style guides (AP, Chicago, Microsoft, Proofpoint), language dialects, and tone settings with configurable parameters for text length limits and API timeouts, it serves content teams needing automated editing workflows, technical writers requiring style guide enforcement, and organizations wanting to integrate enterprise-grade writing assistance into their AI-powered content creation processes.
fields.author: @acrolinx

fields.name: DataHub
fields.description: This MCP server implementation provides a bridge to DataHub, a metadata platform for data ecosystems. Developed by Acryl Data, it integrates with the DataHub API to offer tools for entity retrieval and search functionality across various data assets. The server is designed for use cases requiring metadata management and discovery in complex data environments, such as data cataloging, lineage tracking, and governance.
fields.author: @acryldata

fields.name: ActionsPulse
fields.description: An agentic MCP server for real-time DevOps observability. Integrates as a GitHub App to analyze GitHub Actions, Deployments, and PR health, delivering visual 'State of the Org' reports directly through AI-native interfaces. Features DORA Metrics, Cost Analysis, and CI/CD Health dashboards.
fields.author: @tsviz

fields.name: Activepieces
fields.description: Connect to your hosted MCP Server using any MCP client to communicate with tools
fields.author: @activepieces

fields.name: Actor-Critic Thinking
fields.description: Actor-Critic Thinking MCP Server provides dual-perspective analysis through alternating actor (empathetic/creative) and critic (analytical/evaluative) viewpoints for comprehensive performance evaluation. Developed by Aquarius Wing, it implements a structured thinking methodology that tracks rounds of analysis, balancing subjective understanding with objective assessment to generate nuanced, multi-dimensional evaluations. The server is particularly valuable for evaluating creative works, strategic decisions, and complex scenarios requiring both empathy and objectivity, such as performance reviews, artistic assessments, and iterative creative processes that benefit from constructive feedback combining intention analysis with execution evaluation.
fields.author: @aquarius-wing

fields.name: Actual Budget
fields.description: MCP server for Actual Budget that exposes personal financial data through natural language interactions with AI assistants. Built with TypeScript and the official Actual Budget API, it provides tools for retrieving transactions with filtering, generating spending breakdowns by category, calculating monthly summaries with savings rates, and viewing account balance history over time. The implementation follows a modular architecture with separate core utilities for data fetching, transaction aggregation, and report generation, making it valuable for users who want to analyze their budgets, track spending patterns, and get financial insights without manually navigating the Actual Budget interface.
fields.author: @s-stefanov

fields.name: Doit
fields.description: Provides work planning and tracking capabilities for AI agents through both a CLI and an MCP server interface. Features hierarchical task management (epic, task, subtask), dependency tracking with automatic readiness detection, hash-based IDs to prevent merge conflicts, and multi-tenant support with API keys. Built on PostgreSQL with a Go architecture following proven patterns.
fields.author: @actual-outcomes

fields.name: Acurast
fields.description: Acurast MCP Server provides a bridge to the Acurast decentralized computing platform, enabling AI assistants to deploy scripts and review processor performance. Built with TypeScript and Express, it implements tools for script deployment and resources for retrieving processor metrics, such as count and detailed performance statistics. The server exposes endpoints for handling MCP requests through a StreamableHTTPServerTransport, making it valuable for managing and monitoring distributed computing tasks on the Acurast network.
fields.author: @andreasgassmann

fields.name: Japan Weather
fields.description: This MCP server, developed by acxelerator, provides weather information for Japan. It integrates with an external weather API to retrieve current conditions and forecasts. The implementation is built using Python and leverages libraries like httpx for API requests and pydantic for data validation. It offers a straightforward interface for querying weather data by location within Japan. This server is particularly useful for applications or AI assistants that need up-to-date weather information for Japanese cities, enabling use cases such as travel planning, event scheduling, or local news and information services.
fields.author: @acxelerator

fields.name: Antigravity Sync
fields.description: Provides integration between external AI coding clients and the Antigravity IDE by establishing connections through the Chrome DevTools Protocol. The server exposes tools for asking Antigravity questions, checking quota status, launching the IDE, and managing CDP connections. It includes a companion VS Code/Antigravity sidecar extension that handles CDP discovery and registry management, enabling seamless cross-environment collaboration between AI assistants and the Antigravity development platform.
fields.author: @ada20204

fields.name: Red Hat OpenShift AI
fields.description: Provides tools for interacting with Red Hat OpenShift AI (RHOAI) clusters through Kubernetes APIs, developed as part of a bachelor's thesis project. Built in Go using the official MCP SDK and Kubernetes client libraries, it exposes tools for listing pods and workbenches (Kubeflow notebooks) within specified namespaces, requiring users to be authenticated with their OpenShift cluster via standard kubeconfig. Demonstrates integration between AI assistants and container orchestration platforms, making it useful for DevOps workflows, cluster monitoring, and managing machine learning workloads in OpenShift environments.
fields.author: @ada333

fields.name: AdAdvisor
fields.description: Connects to the AdAdvisor platform for querying Meta Ads performance data. Access campaign metrics, ad set performance, and ad-level analytics. Built by agency veterans with experience managing $60M+ in ad spend, providing AI-driven optimization recommendations for Meta advertising campaigns.
fields.author: @unknown

fields.name: Paper Search
fields.description: A multi-source academic paper search and retrieval server that aggregates results from arXiv, PubMed, bioRxiv, medRxiv, Google Scholar, IACR ePrint Archive, Semantic Scholar, and CrossRef through a unified interface. Built by P.S Zhang with FastMCP, it provides both legacy platform-specific search tools and standardized search/fetch tools compatible with OpenAI Deep Research, featuring PDF download capabilities, text extraction using PyPDF2, intelligent caching with LRU eviction, and asynchronous processing for efficient multi-platform queries. The implementation handles rate limiting, provides consistent Paper object formatting across all sources, and includes containerized deployment support, making it useful for research workflows, literature reviews, and AI-assisted academic research where accessing papers from multiple databases through a single interface streamlines the discovery and analysis process.
fields.author: @adamamer20

fields.name: PokeMCP
fields.description: Connects Claude to the mGBA emulator for playing Pokemon Fire Red. Provides tools for button presses, screenshots, game state reading from RAM, and save state management. Claude handles battles, dialog, and menus while a human operator navigates the overworld.
fields.author: @adamescj

fields.name: Adamik (Blockchain and AI)
fields.description: This MCP server implementation provides a bridge to the Adamik API, enabling AI assistants and other MCP-compatible clients to interact with Adamik's services. Developed by AdamikHQ, it supports integration with Starknet for blockchain operations. The server is designed to work seamlessly with Claude Desktop and other MCP environments, offering a straightforward setup process. It's particularly useful for AI applications requiring access to Adamik's specialized services, such as blockchain interactions or custom AI functionalities provided by the Adamik platform. The implementation focuses on secure API key management and efficient request handling, making it suitable for both development and production environments.
fields.author: @adamikhq

fields.name: Fastmail JMAP by adamisrael
fields.description: Rust-based MCP server providing three tools for Fastmail email access: search with advanced filters, inbox listing with pagination, and full email content retrieval. Requires a Fastmail API token for authentication.
fields.author: @adamisrael

fields.name: MySQL
fields.description: A Node.js-based MySQL MCP server that provides secure database access with multi-database support, lazy loading connection pools, and configurable security levels ranging from read-only operations to full SQL command access. Features hot reloading of database configurations without restart, explicit database parameter requirements to prevent accidental queries, and comprehensive table schema information through SHOW CREATE TABLE commands. Built for developers who need to interact with multiple MySQL databases through AI assistants like Claude and VS Code, with particular emphasis on security through command whitelisting and connection management efficiency through on-demand pool creation.
fields.author: @adamosk

fields.name: CrewAI Workflow
fields.description: A lightweight Python server for running CrewAI multi-agent workflows through the Model Context Protocol. Designed to automatically load agent and task configurations from YAML files, enabling flexible AI task orchestration with minimal custom code. Supports dynamic agent and task creation, variable templating, and seamless integration with tools like Claude Desktop or Cursor IDE.
fields.author: @adam-paterson

fields.name: Multi-Service Gateway
fields.description: This modular MCP server implementation provides a standardized way for AI models to interact with external tools and services through a unified gateway. It includes five specialized tools: GitHub for repository management, GitLab for project interactions, Google Maps for location services, Memory for persistent data storage, and Puppeteer for web automation. Built with Flask and designed for deployment on Red Hat environments, it features containerized deployment options using Podman or Docker, comprehensive error handling, and seamless integration with OpenAI and Anthropic LLMs through standardized request/response formats.
fields.author: @adampippert

fields.name: Nextflow Developer Tools
fields.description: Nextflow Developer Tools MCP provides a suite of tools for Nextflow development and testing, built with FastMCP. It enables AI assistants to interact with a local Nextflow repository to perform tasks like building from source, running tests (integration, specific, and plugin tests), executing the development version, and managing the development directory. The implementation includes comprehensive logging, error handling, and environment variable configuration, making it particularly valuable for developers working on Nextflow pipelines who want to streamline their workflow without leaving their AI assistant interface.
fields.author: @adamrtalbot

fields.name: API Tools
fields.description: This MCP server provides AI assistants with dynamic API integration capabilities by converting JSON configuration files into callable tools, built by Adam Shannag using Go with support for both STDIO and SSE transports. The implementation features flexible request handling with path/query parameter substitution, environment variable templating for secure credential management, comprehensive authentication middleware for SSE deployments, and automatic tool registration from declarative JSON schemas that define endpoints, parameters, and request structures. Built with configurable HTTP clients, structured logging, graceful shutdown handling, and extensive test coverage, it serves developers needing to rapidly expose REST APIs to AI assistants without custom coding, teams requiring secure API access through conversational interfaces, and organizations wanting to bridge existing web services with AI workflows through simple configuration files rather than building dedicated MCP servers for each API integration.
fields.author: @adamshannag

fields.name: Lighthouse (PageSpeed Insights)
fields.description: A Lighthouse MCP server that provides AI assistants with access to Google PageSpeed Insights API data. It enables running performance audits on web pages, retrieving detailed metrics like First Contentful Paint and Largest Contentful Paint, and identifying optimization opportunities. The server exposes a single tool that accepts URL parameters, strategy selection (mobile/desktop), and category filtering, making it valuable for web developers looking to analyze and improve site performance without leaving their AI assistant conversation.
fields.author: @adamsilverstein

fields.name: Flutter Documentation
fields.description: This Flutter MCP server provides AI assistants with real-time access to Flutter, Dart, and pub.dev documentation through on-demand web scraping with SQLite caching, ensuring always up-to-date information without requiring pre-built indexes. The implementation features intelligent URL resolution for Flutter API classes across multiple libraries (widgets, material, cupertino, etc.), comprehensive HTML-to-Markdown processing that extracts constructors, properties, methods, and code examples, and robust error handling with circuit breakers and exponential backoff retry logic. Built with FastMCP and featuring rate limiting for respectful scraping, it supports searching Flutter documentation, retrieving pub.dev package information, and processing @flutter_mcp mentions in prompts, making it valuable for Flutter developers who need current documentation integrated into their AI-powered development workflows.
fields.author: @adamsmaka

fields.name: Resource Hub
fields.description: This Resource Hub MCP server, developed by Adam Wattis, acts as a proxy between local MCP environments and a centralized Resource Hub. It enables access to shared tools, configurations, and resources across different environments, streamlining MCP server management. Built with TypeScript and leveraging the Model Context Protocol SDK, it offers features like centralized configuration, environment variable-based authentication, and compatibility with Claude Desktop. The server includes debugging tools through the MCP Inspector and supports easy deployment via npx. By centralizing resource management, it facilitates consistent tool access and configuration sharing, making it particularly valuable for teams and organizations requiring standardized MCP setups across multiple environments or projects.
fields.author: @adamwattis

fields.name: Router
fields.description: This MCP proxy server, developed by Adam Wattis, aggregates multiple MCP resource servers into a unified interface. It connects to and manages multiple backend servers, exposes their combined capabilities, handles request routing, and aggregates responses. The implementation features resource management across servers, tool aggregation and routing, and multi-server prompt handling. By centralizing access to distributed MCP resources, this proxy enables more flexible and scalable AI assistant deployments. It is particularly useful for organizations managing multiple specialized MCP servers or those looking to modularize their AI infrastructure while maintaining a single point of access for clients.
fields.author: @adamwattis

fields.name: Embedded Debugger (probe-rs)
fields.description: Embedded Debugger MCP server that provides AI assistants with comprehensive hardware debugging capabilities for ARM Cortex-M and RISC-V microcontrollers through probe-rs integration. Built by Adancurusul, the implementation supports popular debug probes including J-Link, ST-Link, DAPLink, and Black Magic Probe, offering 18 debugging tools that cover session management, target control (halt/run/reset/step), memory operations, breakpoint management, flash programming, and Real-Time Transfer (RTT) communication for live firmware debugging. Designed for embedded developers and AI-assisted firmware development workflows who need conversational access to hardware debugging without manual probe configuration, enabling natural language interaction with microcontroller debugging sessions, memory inspection, and firmware flashing operations.
fields.author: @adancurusul

fields.name: Serial Port Communication
fields.description: Serial MCP Server provides comprehensive serial port communication capabilities for AI assistants, enabling discovery, connection management, and data transmission with embedded devices and hardware. Built by Adancurusul using Rust and the rmcp SDK, the implementation supports multiple data encodings (UTF-8, hex, base64), configurable connection parameters (baud rate, parity, flow control), session management with automatic cleanup, and robust error handling with reconnection capabilities. Designed for developers working with IoT devices, embedded systems, or hardware automation who need conversational access to serial communication without manual port configuration, making it valuable for debugging embedded firmware, monitoring sensor data, or controlling hardware devices through natural language interactions.
fields.author: @adancurusul

fields.name: Fabric Pattern Tools
fields.description: The fabric-mcp-server exposes Fabric patterns as tools for integration with Cline through the Model Context Protocol. Built by adapoet, this TypeScript implementation loads patterns from a configurable directory and makes them available as individual tools that can be executed directly within Cline tasks. The server reads each pattern's system.md file and combines it with user input to generate responses, enabling AI-driven pattern execution without leaving the Cline environment. It includes a recommendation feature that suggests appropriate patterns based on task descriptions, making it particularly valuable for developers who want to leverage Fabric's pattern library within their VS Code workflow.
fields.author: @augmentedivan

fields.name: Adaptive Graph of Thoughts
fields.description: This MCP server provides advanced scientific reasoning capabilities through an 8-stage Graph-of-Thoughts pipeline that processes complex queries by decomposing them into hypotheses, gathering evidence, and building knowledge graphs in Neo4j. Built by the Adaptive Graph of Thoughts Development Team using Python with FastAPI, Neo4j, and the official MCP SDK, it implements stages for initialization, decomposition, hypothesis generation, evidence gathering, pruning/merging, subgraph extraction, composition, and reflection with confidence scoring across empirical support, theoretical basis, methodological rigor, and consensus alignment. The implementation supports both HTTP and STDIO transports, includes Docker deployment with Neo4j integration, and features bias detection, falsifiability assessment, and Bayesian confidence updates, making it valuable for researchers conducting systematic literature reviews, scientists analyzing complex interdisciplinary questions, and AI assistants that need structured reasoning with transparent confidence assessments for evidence-based decision making.
fields.author: @saptadey

fields.name: Google Forms
fields.description: Google Forms MCP Server enables AI assistants to create and manage Google Forms through natural language requests. Built with Python and Flask, it features a two-tier architecture with an MCP server handling Google Forms API operations and a CamelAIOrg agent layer that processes natural language into structured form creation commands. The implementation includes real-time visualization of the request flow, comprehensive error handling, and support for various question types. It's particularly useful for automating form creation workflows, enabling quick generation of surveys, feedback forms, and event registrations without requiring direct interaction with the Google Forms interface.
fields.author: @adarshp14

fields.name: Nexus (OpenRouter Search)
fields.description: This Nexus MCP server by adawalli provides AI-powered web search capabilities through OpenRouter's Perplexity Sonar models, enabling intelligent search with source citations directly within MCP-compatible clients like Claude Desktop and Cursor. Built with TypeScript and featuring zero-installation deployment via npx, the implementation includes production-grade features like request deduplication, response caching, comprehensive error handling, and secure API key management with data masking for logs. The server emphasizes developer experience with extensive configuration options, structured logging, retry mechanisms, and high test coverage, making it valuable for developers who need reliable, current web information integrated into their AI workflows without complex setup or maintenance overhead.
fields.author: @adawalli

fields.name: NeuroHive
fields.description: NeuroHive provides shared memory infrastructure for multi-agent systems, enabling agents to track domain expertise, detect conflicting information, and maintain a shared knowledge base. It supports coordination patterns where multiple AI agents collaborate by reading from and writing to a common memory pool with conflict resolution.
fields.author: @adelelo13

fields.name: NeuroMCP
fields.description: NeuroMCP provides AI agents with a persistent semantic memory layer supporting hybrid vector and keyword search, knowledge graph construction, and automatic memory consolidation. It enables agents to store, retrieve, and reason over information across sessions using graph-based memory structures.
fields.author: @adelelo13

fields.name: Geocode
fields.description: Provides geocoding capabilities through OpenStreetMap's Photon service, enabling address-to-coordinate conversion and location-based queries. Built in Rust for performance, offering a lightweight interface to Photon's geocoding API without requiring API keys or rate limit management.
fields.author: @adelie-ai

fields.name: Quran Search Engine
fields.description: Provides Quran search with Arabic normalization, root-based matching, and highlighted results for AI tools to deliver accurate, hallucination-free Quranic text.
fields.author: @adelpro

fields.name: Google Custom Search
fields.description: This MCP server, developed by Allan Denot, provides web search capabilities using the Google Custom Search API. Built with TypeScript and leveraging the Model Context Protocol SDK, it offers a streamlined interface for AI agents to perform web searches. The implementation focuses on simplifying the integration of Google's search functionality into AI workflows, requiring only an API key and search engine ID for setup. It's particularly useful for applications needing up-to-date web information, enabling use cases such as fact-checking, research assistance, and content generation without directly interacting with Google's API complexities.
fields.author: @adenot

fields.name: Shodan
fields.description: Shodan MCP Server provides cybersecurity analysts with direct access to Shodan's network intelligence capabilities through the Model Context Protocol. Developed by ADEO Cybersecurity Services, it exposes a comprehensive set of tools for host information lookup, DNS operations, vulnerability analysis, network scanning, and security alerts management. The implementation includes specialized prompt templates for common cybersecurity workflows like asset discovery, vulnerability assessment, and ICS analysis, making it particularly valuable for security reconnaissance, threat hunting, and infrastructure monitoring without leaving the AI assistant conversation.
fields.author: @adeosec

fields.name: TeamRetro
fields.description: TeamRetro MCP Server provides a bridge between AI assistants and TeamRetro's team management platform, enabling operations like team creation, user management, and member administration through natural language. Built by Ade Pangestu, it implements authentication via API keys, basic auth, or bearer tokens, and formats responses as either simple tables or raw JSON. The server exposes tools for listing, creating, updating, and deleting teams and users, with planned support for retrospectives, action tracking, health checks, and analytics. This unofficial integration is particularly valuable for team leaders who need to manage TeamRetro resources without switching context from their AI assistant conversation.
fields.author: @adepanges

fields.name: VantaGate
fields.description: Provides human-in-the-loop approval workflows for AI agent actions. Integrates with any OpenAPI-compatible service to add human oversight before executing agent operations. Ensures safety and compliance by requiring explicit approval for sensitive automated actions.
fields.author: @aderix

fields.name: Google Workspace
fields.description: MCP server that provides AI assistants with direct access to Google Workspace (Drive, Docs, and Sheets) operations. Built with Python and the MCP protocol, it features OAuth authentication, configurable logging, and support for multiple transport modes (stdio, SSE, WebSocket). The server exposes tools for searching and creating files, managing documents and spreadsheets, and organizing content, making it valuable for users who need to interact with Google Workspace through AI assistants without manual API configuration.
fields.author: @adexltd

fields.name: Raindrop.io
fields.description: The Raindrop.io MCP Server provides AI assistants with direct access to the Raindrop.io bookmarking service through a TypeScript implementation built with Bun. It exposes resources for collections, bookmarks, tags, highlights, and user data, along with tools for CRUD operations, advanced search, tag management, and import/export functionality. The server communicates via stdio by default with optional HTTP/SSE support, uses Axios for API requests, and implements robust error handling with Zod validation. This implementation is particularly valuable for users who want to manage their bookmarks, organize research materials, or analyze saved content without leaving their AI conversation context.
fields.author: @adeze

fields.name: Adfin Financial Management
fields.description: The Adfin MCP server enables Claude to interact with Adfin's financial management platform through its public APIs, allowing users to perform tasks like checking credit control status, creating invoices, and uploading invoice documents. Built with FastMCP, it dynamically generates API tools by importing Adfin's API documentation and registering each endpoint as a separate function, while also providing specialized file system tools for managing invoice uploads from the user's desktop. The implementation includes automatic token refresh handling and is designed to be easily configured in Claude Desktop through environment variables, making it particularly valuable for financial professionals who need to perform Adfin operations without leaving their conversation interface.
fields.author: @adfin-engineering

fields.name: LinkedIn
fields.description: This MCP server, developed by Adhika Setya Pramudita, provides tools to interact with LinkedIn's Feeds and Job API using an unofficial LinkedIn API. It enables users to retrieve feed posts, search for jobs, and analyze job matches against resumes. The implementation uses Python 3.7+ and integrates with libraries like linkedin-api and fastmcp. It's designed for easy configuration through environment variables and can be run using uvicorn. This server is particularly useful for developers building LinkedIn-integrated applications, job search tools, or AI assistants that need to access LinkedIn data and functionality.
fields.author: @adhikasp

fields.name: Git Ingest
fields.description: This MCP server, developed by adhikasp, enables AI assistants like Claude to fetch and analyze GitHub repository structures and contents. Built in Python, it provides capabilities for cloning repositories, generating directory trees, and reading specified files. The implementation stands out by offering deterministic temporary directory creation, Unicode tree representation, and flexible file reading with robust error handling. By connecting AI capabilities with GitHub's vast ecosystem of code repositories, this server allows AI systems to explore codebases, analyze project structures, and access important files programmatically. It is particularly useful for building AI-powered code analysis tools, automated documentation generators, and intelligent assistants for software development workflows that need to interact with GitHub repositories.
fields.author: @adhikasp

fields.name: Twikit
fields.description: This MCP server, developed by adhikasp, integrates with Twitter's API to enable AI assistants to interact with the social media platform. It provides tools for searching tweets, retrieving timelines, and analyzing sentiment across multiple accounts. The server stands out by offering a simplified interface for complex Twitter operations, making it easier for AI systems to process and respond to social media data. By connecting AI capabilities with Twitter's vast user-generated content, this implementation empowers AI assistants to handle tasks like social media monitoring, trend analysis, and customer sentiment tracking. It is particularly useful for applications in brand management, market research, or any scenario where an AI system needs to work with real-time social media data.
fields.author: @adhikasp

fields.name: Reddit
fields.description: This Reddit MCP server, developed by adhikasp, provides AI assistants with a standardized interface to interact with Reddit content. Built using Python and leveraging libraries like PRAW and RedditWarp, it offers capabilities for fetching hot threads, detailed post content, and comments from any subreddit. The server supports different post types including text, links, and galleries. It's designed for flexibility, allowing easy installation and usage through the MCP client CLI. This implementation is particularly valuable for AI assistants focused on social media analysis, content curation, and trend monitoring, enabling use cases such as summarizing popular discussions, tracking emerging topics, and analyzing user engagement across Reddit communities.
fields.author: @adhikasp

fields.name: Twitter
fields.description: MCP-Twikit provides Twitter search and interaction capabilities for AI assistants through the FastMCP framework. Built by Adhika Setya Pramudita, it leverages the Twikit library to enable both read operations (searching tweets, viewing timelines, retrieving user tweets) and write operations (posting tweets, sending direct messages) with built-in rate limiting. The implementation authenticates using Twitter credentials stored as environment variables, manages cookies for persistent sessions, and formats tweet data in markdown for easy readability, making it ideal for workflows requiring Twitter monitoring, social media analysis, or automated Twitter interactions.
fields.author: @zo-valentine

fields.name: YouTube Transcript
fields.description: This MCP server, developed by adhikasp, enables AI assistants like Claude to fetch and analyze YouTube video transcripts. Built in Python, it provides capabilities for downloading subtitles from YouTube videos using either video IDs or full URLs, and includes timestamp information. The implementation stands out by offering a standardized interface to YouTube's transcript data, allowing AI systems to easily access and process video content. By connecting AI capabilities with YouTube's vast repository of video transcripts, this server enables automated content analysis, subtitle generation, and video summarization. It is particularly useful for building AI-powered tools for video research, content moderation, and educational applications that leverage YouTube's extensive library.
fields.author: @adhikasp

fields.name: Core Lightning
fields.description: This MCP server provides direct access to Core Lightning (CLN) nodes through their gRPC interface, enabling AI assistants to query Lightning Network node information, channel states, payment history, and network topology. Built in Rust with TLS certificate authentication and comprehensive error handling, it exposes 25+ Lightning Network operations including node info retrieval, channel management, payment tracking, invoice handling, bookkeeping data, and network analysis through tools like list_peers, get_info, list_channels, and bkpr_channels_apy. The implementation uses the official CLN gRPC client library with async support and includes detailed documentation for each Lightning Network command, making it valuable for Lightning Network operators monitoring node performance, developers building Lightning applications, or financial analysis of routing fees and channel profitability.
fields.author: @adi2011

fields.name: Orthanc DICOM
fields.description: Connects to Orthanc DICOM servers to enable hierarchical querying of medical imaging data. Supports searching for patients by name, ID, or birth date, retrieving imaging studies, accessing series within studies, and querying individual DICOM objects. Includes PDF text extraction from encapsulated DICOM reports for radiology automation workflows.
fields.author: @adiking117

fields.name: Feature Suggestions (Supabase)
fields.description: The Supabase MCP Server connects AI assistants to a Supabase database, allowing them to query feature suggestions stored in the database. Built with TypeScript using the Model Context Protocol SDK, it provides a single tool called 'query_feature_suggestions' that retrieves records from the feature_suggestions table with optional limit parameters. The server includes robust error handling and logging capabilities, making it ideal for workflows where AI assistants need to access, analyze, or present feature request data directly within conversations without requiring manual database queries.
fields.author: @adiletd

fields.name: gRPC Bridge
fields.description: GRPCMCP is a proxy server that bridges AI assistants to gRPC backends using the Model Context Protocol. Developed by Adiom Data, it dynamically exposes gRPC service methods as MCP tools by either using server reflection or pre-compiled protocol buffer descriptors. The server supports both Server-Sent Events (SSE) and STDIN transport methods, handles authentication through bearer tokens, and allows custom header configuration. This implementation is particularly valuable for organizations that want to expose their existing gRPC services to AI assistants without building custom MCP servers for each service.
fields.author: @adiom-data

fields.name: Corporate Dystopia Toolkit
fields.description: Hilanet MCP provides a satirical corporate dystopia toolkit built with fastMCP, offering HR-related tools that parody toxic workplace behaviors. The implementation includes functions for requesting salary increases, filing manager complaints, scheduling inconvenient meetings, generating corporate jargon, and other humorous workplace sabotage actions. Each tool accepts specific parameters like employee names, percentages, or justifications, creating a playful commentary on office politics. The server can be run directly with npx or through npm scripts, making it easily accessible for users wanting to explore this tongue-in-cheek take on corporate culture through AI assistants.
fields.author: @adird

fields.name: Video Edit (MoviePy)
fields.description: A comprehensive video editing MCP server built with MoviePy that provides extensive video and audio processing capabilities including trimming, merging, resizing, cropping, rotation, speed adjustment, format conversion, and effects like fade in/out, grayscale conversion, and mirroring. The implementation features an in-memory object store system for chaining operations efficiently, supports YouTube video downloading via yt-dlp, and includes advanced features like text/image overlays, frame extraction, video splitting, and audio manipulation tools such as volume adjustment, looping, and track mixing. Designed for automated video editing workflows, it's particularly valuable for content creators, developers building video processing applications, and AI assistants that need to perform complex video editing tasks programmatically without manual intervention.
fields.author: @aditya2755

fields.name: Scholarly (arXiv)
fields.description: This mcp-scholarly server, developed by Aditya Karnam, provides a standardized interface for searching academic articles through the arXiv database. Built with Python and leveraging the arxiv library, it offers a tool to query arXiv based on keywords. The server integrates seamlessly with Claude Desktop, abstracting the complexities of academic search into a consistent MCP interface. By enabling AI systems to access and analyze scholarly literature, this implementation facilitates use cases such as literature reviews, research assistance, and staying up-to-date with the latest publications in specific fields. The server is designed for easy deployment and debugging, making it valuable for researchers and developers building AI-powered tools that interact with academic content.
fields.author: @adityak74

fields.name: Apache Solr
fields.description: This Spring Boot MCP server provides Apache Solr search and indexing capabilities through six specialized tools including advanced search with filtering/faceting/pagination, schema-less document indexing from JSON, collection management with health monitoring and statistics, and schema introspection. Built using Java 21 with SolrJ client library and Spring AI MCP framework, it features robust error handling with individual document fallback during batch failures, automatic JSON flattening for nested objects, dynamic field type detection, and comprehensive metrics collection from Solr MBeans including cache performance and handler statistics. The implementation supports both standalone Solr and SolrCloud deployments with Docker Compose setup, making it valuable for AI-assisted search application development, data ingestion workflows, index monitoring and optimization, and building intelligent search interfaces that leverage Solr's full-text search capabilities without requiring direct Solr API knowledge.
fields.author: @adityamparikh

fields.name: BrowserControl
fields.description: BrowserControl is a browser automation server that enables interaction with web pages through a Set of Marks (SoM) system, where screenshots display numbered interactive elements for precise clicking and interaction. Built using Playwright and FastMCP, it provides persistent browser sessions with automatic cookie and login state management, developer tools integration for console logs and network monitoring, session recording capabilities, and smart navigation that handles localhost connection issues. The implementation is useful for web scraping, form filling, testing workflows, or any task requiring visual web interaction with debugging capabilities.
fields.author: @adityasasidhar

fields.name: SkillSync
fields.description: Gates all skill installations behind comprehensive threat detection, scanning GitHub repositories against 60+ threat patterns across categories including prompt injection, reverse shells, credential theft, supply chain attacks, and cryptocurrency mining. Critical threats are permanently blocked while medium/high risk installations require explicit force override. Provides search and AI-powered discovery of skills from the SkillsMP marketplace, cloud sync capabilities, and compatibility across multiple MCP clients including Claude Code, OpenClaw, Cursor, Windsurf, GitHub Copilot, Zed, and nanobot.
fields.author: @adityasugandhi

fields.name: Mail IMAP/SMTP
fields.description: Full-featured email management server supporting IMAP operations (folder management, search, read/unread marking, move/copy) and SMTP sending (with attachments, replies, forwards). Supports SSL, STARTTLS, and OAuth2 authentication across major providers including Gmail, Outlook, and Chinese enterprise email services.
fields.author: @adjia

fields.name: AutoBuild
fields.description: This MCP server provides AI assistants with profile-based build automation capabilities, featuring asynchronous build queuing, file system watching with debounced triggers, and comprehensive build lifecycle management. Built using Python with FastMCP, psutil for process management, and watchdog for file monitoring, it supports concurrent build execution (default: 2), persistent state management through JSON storage, and automatic build triggering when project files change with configurable ignore patterns to prevent recursion. The implementation includes tools for build configuration, manual and automatic build triggering, status monitoring, log retrieval with tailing support, and process termination, along with systemd service integration for Linux deployment and cross-platform batch/shell scripts for setup, making it valuable for development workflows requiring automated builds, continuous integration scenarios, and building AI assistants that need programmatic access to build systems without manual intervention.
